name: Jalapeno Poppers
recipeIngredient: 2 jalapeño peppers, cut in half length-wise and cleaned out of stem, webbing, and seeds
Egg wash (1 egg beaten with a few tablespoons water)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the jalapeño peppers in half length-wise and clean out the stem, webbing, and seeds. Wear gloves to avoid burning your fingers.
2. Grate jack cheese, cheddar cheese, and cream cheese into thumb-sized pieces that will fit into the pepper halves.
3. Stuff the pepper halves with an equal mixture of grated cheeses.
4. Beat one egg with a few tablespoons water for egg wash.
5. Dip the stuffed peppers in the egg wash and coat them with finely ground bread crumbs. Let them dry briefly, then apply a second coating.
6. Heat medium-hot lard (or vegetable oil if necessary) in a frying pan or skillet.
7. Fry the coated jalapeño peppers until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
8. Allow to cool slightly before serving. The poppers will not reheat well once the coating becomes soggy.
